verdict_reasoning: CRISP1 is a secreted protein that stably associates with the sperm surface during epididymal maturation via two populations with different binding affinities—a loosely bound form released during capacitation and a strongly bound form that persists and participates in zona pellucida binding and gamete fusion. However, this surface accessibility is restricted to sperm (and transiently to oocytes during fertilization), making it a tissue/cell-type-restricted surface protein characteristic of the male gamete. Not a general somatic cell surface protein.
